questions.q: In which scientific field is Obaid Siddiqi best known as a pioneer in India?
questions.explanation: Obaid Siddiqi is widely regarded as a founder of molecular biology research in India.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi is credited with founding which Bengaluru-based research institution?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi founded the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) in Bengaluru.

questions.q: What was Obaid Siddiqi's nationality?
questions.explanation: Obaid Siddiqi was an Indian scientist.

questions.q: Besides molecular biology, Obaid Siddiqi is especially known for pioneering which area in India?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was a pioneer of neurogenetics, studying the genetic basis of behaviour and the nervous system.

questions.q: At which major Indian research institute did Obaid Siddiqi establish a molecular biology group before NCBS?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi founded the Molecular Biology Unit at the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR) in Mumbai.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi used which small insect as a model organism for much of his genetics research?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi worked extensively with the fruit fly Drosophila to study genetics and the nervous system.

questions.q: The NCBS that Obaid Siddiqi founded is affiliated with which parent institution?
options: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: NCBS is a centre of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR).

questions.q: Which of these best describes Obaid Siddiqi's career role?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was a research scientist who also built and led major scientific institutions in India.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi's pioneering work helped establish which broad discipline in India during the 1960s?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was instrumental in establishing modern genetics and molecular biology research in India.

questions.q: In which city is the National Centre for Biological Sciences, founded by Siddiqi, located?
questions.explanation: NCBS is located in Bengaluru (Bangalore), Karnataka.

questions.q: In what year was Obaid Siddiqi born?
questions.explanation: Obaid Siddiqi was born on 7 January 1932 in Basti, United Provinces (now Uttar Pradesh).

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi died in 2013 following injuries from what kind of incident?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi died in July 2013 after being injured in a road accident in Bengaluru.

questions.q: Early in his career, Siddiqi did important molecular genetics work on which model organisms before turning to Drosophila?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi did notable early molecular genetics research on microorganisms such as fungi (Aspergillus) and bacteria before focusing on Drosophila.

questions.q: At which American institution did Obaid Siddiqi spend a major formative research period working on bacterial genetics with Alan Garen?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi did influential work on suppressor mutations and the genetic code with Alan Garen at the University of Pennsylvania.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi's neurogenetics research in Drosophila focused heavily on which sensory modalities?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi pioneered the genetic analysis of taste and smell (chemosensory behaviour) in the fruit fly.

questions.q: Which prestigious British scientific society elected Obaid Siddiqi as a Fellow?
questions.explanation: Obaid Siddiqi was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) of London.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi received the Padma Vibhushan, which is which rank among India's civilian honours?
questions.explanation: The Padma Vibhushan is India's second-highest civilian award; Siddiqi received it in 2006.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi was a founding figure of the Molecular Biology Unit at TIFR around which decade?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi established TIFR's Molecular Biology Unit in the 1960s after returning to India.

questions.q: Which famous physicist and TIFR founder recruited Obaid Siddiqi to start molecular biology research in India?
questions.explanation: Homi J. Bhabha, founder of TIFR, invited Siddiqi to establish molecular biology research at the institute.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi's work on temperature-sensitive paralytic mutants of Drosophila contributed to understanding what?
questions.explanation: His para temperature-sensitive paralytic mutants helped illuminate the genetics of nerve conduction and synaptic transmission.

questions.q: Apart from FRS, Obaid Siddiqi was also elected a foreign associate/member of which body?
options: The US National Academy of Sciences
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was elected a Foreign Associate of the US National Academy of Sciences in recognition of his research.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Obaid Siddiqi born?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was born in Basti, in what is now Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi's early genetics PhD research, completed in Britain, was carried out on which fungus?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi earned his PhD studying genetic recombination in the fungus Aspergillus nidulans at the University of Glasgow.

questions.q: Siddiqi and Alan Garen's classic work on suppressor mutations helped reveal the nature of which fundamental genetic phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Their study of suppressors of nonsense mutations in E. coli alkaline phosphatase clarified how suppressor tRNAs override chain-terminating codons.

questions.q: At which Scottish university did Obaid Siddiqi obtain his doctorate?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi received his PhD from the University of Glasgow in Scotland.

questions.q: Which TIFR-based research unit, founded by Siddiqi, was the direct institutional precursor that grew into NCBS?
questions.explanation: NCBS grew out of the Molecular Biology Unit that Siddiqi had established at TIFR.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi's pioneering genetic dissection of Drosophila taste used mutants defective in detecting which class of stimuli?
questions.explanation: He isolated and characterized gustatory mutants impaired in responding to sugars and other taste stimuli, founding the genetics of taste in flies.

questions.q: Obaid Siddiqi shared a deep scientific collaboration and friendship with which Nobel-laureate molecular biologist known for work on the genetic code and C. elegans?
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was a close collaborator and friend of Sydney Brenner, who strongly supported the founding of NCBS.

questions.q: Which of these honours did Obaid Siddiqi actually receive in recognition of his scientific contributions?
options: Fellowship of the Third World Academy of Sciences (TWAS)
questions.explanation: Siddiqi was elected a Fellow of the Third World Academy of Sciences (TWAS, now The World Academy of Sciences); he did not receive the Fields Medal, the Turing Award, or the Bharat Ratna.

questions.q: Which statement most accurately captures Obaid Siddiqi's lasting significance to Indian science?
options: He built world-class genetics and neuroscience research and trained generations of biologists in India
He designed India's first satellite launch vehicle
He discovered a new fundamental particle
questions.explanation: Siddiqi's enduring legacy is establishing world-class molecular biology and neurogenetics in India and mentoring many leading biologists through TIFR and NCBS.
